activity: cycling instead of driving 10km
note: Cycling has near-zero operational emissions compared to an average passenger car which emits about 240g CO2 per kilometer.

activity: taking a public train instead of driving 50km
note: Train travel reduces greenhouse gas emissions by around 80% compared to driving a standard single-occupancy car.

activity: flying economy class for a 2-hour domestic flight
note: A domestic flight emits approximately 150 to 250 kg of CO2e per passenger due to aviation fuel burn.

activity: driving an electric vehicle for 100km
note: An electric vehicle emits about 50g of CO2e per kilometer based on average grid electricity mix.

activity: switching to 100% renewable electricity for one month
note: Transitioning to wind or solar power eliminates the emissions from fossil fuel-based electricity generation.

activity: using a clothesline to dry laundry instead of a tumble dryer
note: Air-drying clothes saves about 1.5 to 3 kg of CO2e per load by avoiding electricity consumption.

activity: lowering the home thermostat by 1 degree Celsius in winter
note: Lowering your heating by 1 degree Celsius can reduce energy bills and carbon emissions by up to 10% annually.

activity: using LED light bulbs instead of incandescent for 5 hours daily for a year
note: LED bulbs use up to 80% less energy than traditional incandescent bulbs, preventing significant carbon emissions over their lifespan.

activity: eating a plant-based vegan diet for one week
note: A plant-based diet reduces carbon footprint by about 2.5 kg CO2e per day compared to a meat-heavy diet.

activity: replacing beef with chicken for 1kg of meat
note: Producing beef emits roughly 27 kg of CO2e per kg, whereas chicken produces around 6 kg.

activity: wasting 1kg of edible food
note: Food waste in landfills decays anaerobically, producing methane which has a high global warming potential.

activity: recycling 10kg of paper and cardboard
note: Recycling paper saves energy and prevents methane emissions that occur when paper decomposes in landfills.

activity: composting organic kitchen waste for one month
note: Composting organic matter aerobically avoids the methane production associated with landfill decomposition.

activity: using reusable shopping bags for a year
note: Substituting single-use plastic bags with reusable ones prevents plastic manufacturing emissions and waste accumulation.

activity: reducing daily shower time from 10 minutes to 5 minutes for a week
note: Shorter showers save heated water, reducing the natural gas or electricity required by the water heater.

activity: harvesting and using rainwater for garden watering
note: Using rainwater reduces the energy consumed by municipal water treatment and pumping facilities.

activity: running the dishwasher only when fully loaded
note: A full dishwasher load optimizes both water heating energy and water consumption compared to multiple partial loads.

activity: buying a new smartphone instead of keeping the old one
note: Around 80% of a smartphone's carbon footprint is generated during its manufacturing phase.

activity: buying secondhand clothing instead of new garments
note: Extending the life of clothes by buying secondhand reduces manufacturing, transportation, and textile waste emissions.

activity: streaming video in standard definition instead of 4K for 10 hours
note: Lower resolution video streams require less data transmission and processing power in data centers and networks.
